page: OVHcloud b2-15 is a general-purpose virtual server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with 4 dedicated physical cores and 4 vCPUs, utilizing an Intel Haswell processor clocked at 2.0 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 15.0 GB of system memory, providing 3.75 GB per core, alongside a bundled 100 GB local SSD for storage. The CPU features 32 KB of L1d cache, 4 MB of L2 cache, and 16 MB of L3 cache, with support for AVX, AES, BMI2, and FMA instruction sets, though it lacks AVX-512 and AMX capabilities.

In benchmark evaluations, the instance demonstrates entry-level performance profiles. The single-core and multi-core CPU performance, measured via stress-ng and Geekbench, falls into the bottom 10% and 25% categories respectively. Memory bandwidth for small cached blocks is average, reaching 347.69 GB/sec, while large-block read and write speeds are weaker, registering at 37.17 GB/sec and 22.32 GB/sec. Database operations and Redis SET operations show average performance, with Redis achieving 5.93 million operations per second. Small-scale LLM inference using a 135M model performs at an average tier, but processing larger models like the 7B parameter model results in poor performance.

The server offers a balanced resource allocation for general-purpose workloads that do not require high-performance compute or high-speed network baselines, as baseline network bandwidth is rated at 0 Gbps. It represents a cost-efficient option for low-intensity applications, development environments, basic database operations, and lightweight web serving. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent performance without resource sharing, though the older Haswell architecture limits its suitability for modern high-performance computing, large language model inference, or heavy compilation tasks.
description: OVHcloud b2-15 is a general-purpose virtual server built on the KVM hypervisor. It is equipped with 4 dedicated vCPUs on an Intel Haswell x86_64 architecture running at 2.0 GHz, 15.0 GB of RAM, and a bundled 100 GB local SSD. Performance benchmarks indicate entry-level capabilities, with single-core and multi-core CPU metrics falling into the lower percentiles. However, memory bandwidth for small cached blocks and database operations perform at an average level, with Redis SET operations reaching 5.93 million operations per second. Lacking high-speed baseline network bandwidth and advanced CPU instructions like AVX-512, this instance is best suited for low-intensity workloads, development environments, basic database hosting, and lightweight web serving where dedicated CPU resources are required without the need for high-performance computing.
og_description: OVHcloud b2-15 is a general-purpose virtual server featuring 4 dedicated Intel Haswell vCPUs, 15 GB of RAM, and a 100 GB local SSD, designed for entry-level workloads and development environments.
meta_description: OVHcloud b2-15 is a general-purpose KVM virtual server with 4 dedicated Intel vCPUs, 15 GB RAM, and a 100 GB SSD for entry-level workloads.
tagline: A dedicated four-core virtual server with balanced memory and local SSD storage for entry-level workloads and development environments.
bullet_points: 4 dedicated Intel Haswell vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz
15.0 GB of system memory and 100 GB local SSD storage
Average performance for database and Redis SET operations
Includes one complimentary public IPv4 address
Suited for development environments and lightweight web serving
